localizedDescriptions.en: A persistent and evolving summary record of information about lifestyle risk factors that may influence health outcomes, clinical decision-making and provision of care.
Metadata associated with the documents within the XDM message (XDSDocumentEntry) as defined in the NEHTA CDA Packaging Specification.
Date and/or Time the document was created in the system of origin.
Unique ID for this event from the CIS (note, source of info is the SEHR).
Unique ID for this event from the CIS (note, source of info is the SEHR).
Unique ID for this event from the CIS (note, source of info is the SEHR).
Summary or persistent information about the tobacco smoking habits of an individual.
Statement about current smoking behaviour for all types of tobacco.
Individual has never smoked any type of tobacco.
Individual is a current smoker of tobacco.
Individual has previously smoked tobacco but is not a current smoker.
Narrative summary about the individual's overall tobacco smoking pattern and history.

annotations.comment: Use this data element to record a narrative description of the tobacco smoking habits for this individual or to incorporate unstructured tobacco smoking information from existing or legacy clinical systems into an archetyped format.

localizedDescriptions.en: The date or partial date when the individual first started frequent or regular, but usually non-daily, smoking of tobacco of any type.

annotations.comment: Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. For example, this date could represent when the individual commenced smoking every Friday night or at parties.

localizedDescriptions.en: The date or partial date when the individual first started daily smoking of tobacco of any type.

annotations.comment: Can be a partial date, for example, only a year.

localizedDescriptions.en: Details about smoking activity for a specified type of smoked tobacco.
The type of tobacco smoked by the individual.
Also known as manufactured cigarettes, 'factory made' cigarettes or 'tailor made' cigarettes. Processed tobacco, manufactured into cylinder made of paper or a substance that does not contain tobacco.
Also known as "rollies" or "roll-ups". Loose tobacco, hand rolled into a cylinder using cigarette papers.
Also known as "large cigar". Roll of tobacco wrapped within a leaf tobacco or in a substance that contains tobacco.
Also known as mini cigars. Short and narrow cigar.
Loose tobacco placed inside a pipe bowl.
Also known as "hookah", "shisha", "narguileh" and "hubble-bubble". Tobacco, often flavoured, is burned then cooled through a basin of water and consumed through a hose and mouthpiece.
Also known as Beedis. Thin hand-rolled cigarettes filled with tobacco and wrapped in a leaf, often tied with colorful string at one or both ends. They can be flavoured or unflavoured.
Also known as clove cigarettes. Cigarettes that contain a mixture of tobacco, cloves and other additives.
Statement about current smoking behaviour for the specified type of tobacco.
Individual is a current smoker of the specified type of tobacco.
Individual has previously smoked the specified type of tobacco but is not a current smoker.
Individual has never smoked the specified type of tobacco.
Narrative summary about smoking behaviour for the specified type of tobacco.
Details about a discrete period of smoking activity for the specified type of tobacco.
Identification of an episode of smoking activity - either as a number in a sequence and/or a named event.
Identification of an episode of smoking activity - either as a number in a sequence and/or a named event.

annotations.comment: For example: '2' as the second episode within a sequence of episodes; or 'Pregnancy with twins' if describing the smoking activity during a health event such as during a specific pregnancy.

localizedDescriptions.en: Identification of an episode of smoking activity - either as a number in a sequence and/or a named event.

annotations.comment: For example: '2' as the second episode within a sequence of episodes; or 'Pregnancy with twins' if describing the smoking activity during a health event such as during a specific pregnancy.
For example: '2' as the second episode within a sequence of episodes; or 'Pregnancy with twins' if describing the smoking activity during a health event such as during a specific pregnancy.

localizedDescriptions.en: Date when this episode commenced.

annotations.comment: Can be a partial date, for example, only a year.

localizedDescriptions.en: Date when this episode ceased.

annotations.comment: Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. This data field will be empty if the episode is current and ongoing.

localizedDescriptions.en: The typical pattern of smoking for the specified type of tobacco.
Smoking the specified type of tobacco at least once every day.
Not smoking the specified type of tobacco every day.
Estimate of number of units of the specified type of tobacco consumed.

annotations.comment: For example: the number of 'sticks' or 'full pipes' per day or per week. This data element is redundant if a value is recorded for 'Typical use(mass)'.

localizedDescriptions.en: Estimate of the weight of loose leaf tobacco smoked.

annotations.comment: This data element will typically be used for pipes and hand-rolled cigarettes and is redundant if a value is recorded for 'Typical use (units)'.

localizedDescriptions.en: Total number of times the individual has attempted to stop smoking the specified type of tobacco within this episode.
Additional narrative about tobacco smoking during the specified episode, not captured in other fields.
Date when the individual last smoked the specified type of tobacco.

annotations.comment: Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. Definitions for a 'Quit date' vary enormously and can be defined using the 'Quit data definition' data element in the Protocol section of this archetype. This date will be identical to the 'Episode end date' for the most recent episode. This date could be used by decision support guidance to determine if the  individual is at risk of relapse, for example in the first 12 months since quitting.

localizedDescriptions.en: Estimate of the cumulative amount of tobacco smoked using the specified type of tobacco.

annotations.comment: Commonly used in assessment of cigarette use. It is calculated by multiplying the number of packs of cigarettes smoked per day by the number of years the individual has smoked. One pack year equals 365 packs of cigarettes. Details about how to calculate pack years for other types of tobacco can be found at http://smokingpackyears.com. The definition of a pack can be recorded in the protocol of this archetype using the 'Pack definition' data element.

localizedDescriptions.en: Additional narrative about smoking of the specified type of tobacco, not captured in other fields.
The date when the individual last ceased using tobacco of any type.

annotations.comment: Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. This date could be used by decision support guidance to determine if the  individual is at risk of relapse, for example in the first 12 months since quitting.

localizedDescriptions.en: The cumulative number of years that the individual has smoked tobacco.

annotations.comment: This data element does not take into account the amount of tobacco smoked. It may be used to calculate the 'Smoking index'.

localizedDescriptions.en: An indication of the cumulative amount of tobacco smoking exposure.

annotations.comment: This parameter is similar to Pack Years but based on units of cigarettes, bidis etc smoked per day, rather than packs.

localizedDescriptions.en: Estimate of the cumulative amount for all types of tobacco smoked.

annotations.comment: The definition of a pack can be recorded in the protocol of this archetype using the 'Pack definition' data element.

localizedDescriptions.en: Additional narrative about all tobacco smoking that has not been captured in other fields.

annotations.comment: For example: stopped smoking or reduced amount on becoming pregnant.

localizedDescriptions.en: The applied definition for the 'Quit date' data elements used in this archetype.
The applied definition for a Quit attempt used to determine value for the 'Number of quit attempts' data element used in this archetype.

annotations.comment: For example: 'stopped smoking for one day or longer with the intention of quitting'.

localizedDescriptions.en: The applied definition for the 'Current smoker' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.

annotations.comment: Definition may need to be specified per type. Current smoker definitions vary in different jurisdictions. For example: USA Centre for Disease Control refers to smoking during the past one month and New Zealand Ministry of Health uses 28 days.

localizedDescriptions.en: The applied definition for the 'Former smoker' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.

annotations.comment: Definition may need to be specified per type of tobacco. Former smoker definitions vary in different jurisdictions. For example: New Zealand Ministry of Health refers to not having smoked during the past 28 days.

localizedDescriptions.en: The applied definition for the 'Never smoked' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.

annotations.comment: Definition may need to be specified per type. For example, the definition may not be zero, but less than a specified amount (as units or mass) smoked during a specified time interval.

localizedDescriptions.en: The definition of the size of pack used as part of the algorithm for calculating 'Pack years' data elements used in this archetype.
The definition of the size of pack used as part of the algorithm for calculating 'Pack years' data elements used in this archetype.

annotations.comment: Definition may need to be specified per type. For example, the number of units in a pack used for cigarettes may be different to cigars; the mass of loose tobacco in a pack used for hand-rolled cigarettes or pipes.

localizedDescriptions.en: The definition of the size of pack used as part of the algorithm for calculating 'Pack years' data elements used in this archetype.

annotations.comment: Definition may need to be specified per type. For example, the number of units in a pack used for cigarettes may be different to cigars; the mass of loose tobacco in a pack used for hand-rolled cigarettes or pipes.

Definition may need to be specified per type. For example, the number of units in a pack used for cigarettes may be different to cigars; the mass of loose tobacco in a pack used for hand-rolled cigarettes or pipes.

localizedDescriptions.en: The date this tobacco smoking summary was last updated.
